glb_set_investment: Not quantified in source files — 5 trials

what_ceacam5_is.biology: CEACAM5 is a GPI-anchored glycoprotein overexpressed on the surface of colorectal, lung, gastric, and pancreatic cancer cells. It mediates cell-cell adhesion, promotes invasion and metastasis, and suppresses immune recognition by binding inhibitory receptors on NK cells and T cells. In normal tissue, CEACAM5 expression is restricted to the apical surface of intestinal epithelium — making it a tumor-selective target with a therapeutic window.
what_ceacam5_is.why_it_matters_in_crc: CEACAM5 is expressed in 98.7% of CRC tumors (Jansen et al. Cancers 2024). MSS CRC has HIGHER CEACAM5 expression than MSI-H CRC (MMR deficiency reduces CEA production, P<0.0001). This makes CEACAM5 one of the most tumor-selective targets in MSS mCRC — the challenge is not target expression but patient selection threshold.
what_ceacam5_is.serum_cea_as_proxy: Serum CEA (cCEA) is a liquid biopsy proxy for CEACAM5 IHC expression. cCEA ≥100 ng/mL correlates with IHC high-expressing tumors (Spearman ρ=0.43, n=92). In NSCLC: cCEA ≥100 ORR 41.7% vs 8.1% (P=0.003) in CEACAM5-high patients.

two_gate_architecture.description: CrisPRO's two-gate patient selection framework for CEACAM5-targeted programs

gate_1.name: CEACAM5 Expression Gate
gate_1.threshold: IHC ≥80% (H-score or percentage of cells staining ≥2+)
gate_1.rationale: CARMEN-LC03 enrolled at IHC ≥50% — this captured 72% of patients who could not respond. The ≥80% subgroup showed directional OS benefit (HR=0.71, post-hoc). The ≥50–79% subgroup showed numerically harmful PFS (HR=1.38). The gate must be ≥80% to enrich for responders.
gate_1.population_fraction: ~28% of MSS mCRC patients (OPEN_ASSUMPTION OA-08 — CARMEN-LC03 NSCLC proxy; CRC-specific data not available)
gate_1.liquid_biopsy_proxy: cCEA ≥100 ng/mL (NSCLC data only — CRC application by inference)
gate_1.crc_caveat: In PROCEADE-CRC-01 (N=40), IHC Spearman rho=−0.14 (P=0.4) — IHC is NOT predictive in CRC at any threshold tested. The second gate is mandatory for CRC programs.

gate_2.name: IO Permissiveness Gate
gate_2.threshold: pTMB ≥28 OR Immunoscore-IC high OR CD8+ TIL density high
gate_2.rationale: CEACAM5-targeted ADCs kill tumor cells but do not activate the immune system. In MSS CRC, the tumor microenvironment is immunosuppressive — killed cells are not immunogenic without an inflamed TME. Gate 2 selects patients whose TME can amplify the ADC's cytotoxic effect through immune activation.
gate_2.why_gate_2_is_mandatory_in_crc: PROCEADE-CRC-01 showed IHC is not predictive in CRC. The responder subgroup (3/40 = 7.5% ORR) likely had both high CEACAM5 expression AND an inflamed TME. Without Gate 2, the signal is diluted by immunosuppressed patients.

vector_scalpel_ranking.description: 11-dimension biomarker ranking for CEACAM5 patient selection — which biomarker best identifies the responder subpopulation

ranking.rationale: Liquid biopsy — no biopsy required, real-time monitoring, correlates with IHC
Strongest IO enrichment signal in MSS CRC from randomized data (CO.26 OS HR=0.34)
Tissue-based — requires biopsy, archival tissue may not reflect current expression
CD8+ TIL density at tumor center and invasive margin

trials.primary_result: PFS HR 1.14 (95% CI 0.86–1.51; P=0.8204) — FAILED primary endpoint

D1_biology.rationale: CEACAM5 is a genuine surface target — biology is sound. Low D1 score because the target is real.

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. IHC ≥50% threshold enrolled 72% of patients who could not respond. The ≥50–79% subgroup showed numerically harmful PFS (HR=1.38). The responder subgroup (IHC ≥80%) was diluted by non-responders. This is the canonical D2 Selection failure.

D3_architecture.rationale: Phase III design was appropriate — randomized, powered, pre-specified endpoint.

D4_timing.rationale: 2L+ timing is appropriate for this mechanism.

D6_resistance.rationale: DM4 payload resistance possible but not the primary driver.

D8_systemic.rationale: No safety hold or regulatory issue.

trials.responder_result: OS HR=0.71 (post-hoc, directional — CI not published; underpowered)
trials.transfer_lesson: Set IHC ≥80% as the enrollment gate. Add IO permissiveness as Gate 2. Consider cCEA ≥100 as liquid biopsy screening tool.

D1_biology.rationale: CEACAM5 is expressed in 98.7% of CRC — biology is sound.

D2_selection.rationale: IHC Spearman rho=−0.14 (P=0.4) — IHC is NOT predictive in CRC at any threshold. The 3 responders likely had both high CEACAM5 AND inflamed TME. Without Gate 2 (IO permissiveness), the signal is diluted.

D3_architecture.rationale: Phase II single-arm — appropriate for signal-finding.

D6_resistance.rationale: MSS CRC TME is immunosuppressive — DM4 payload may not trigger immunogenic cell death in this context.

D7_translational.rationale: NSCLC data does not directly translate to CRC — different TME biology.

trials.key_finding: IHC is not predictive in CRC — Gate 2 (IO permissiveness) is mandatory
trials.transfer_lesson: For CRC CEACAM5 programs: IHC alone is insufficient. Must add IO permissiveness gate (pTMB ≥28 or Immunoscore-IC high).

trials.primary_result: ORR 1.2% (1/86 confirmed PR); mPFS 3.6 months; mOS 6.9 months

D2_selection.rationale: Unselected population — no IHC threshold, no IO gate.

D6_resistance.rationale: SN-38 payload — topoisomerase I inhibitor. Resistance mechanisms well-characterized. Govitecan (SN-38) is less potent than Topo1i payloads in newer ADCs.

D7_translational.rationale: Older ADC technology — linker stability and bystander effect inferior to newer platforms.

trials.transfer_lesson: Payload matters as much as target. SN-38 is inferior to DM4 and Topo1i for CEACAM5 ADCs. Next-generation programs (SAR445953 — Topo1i payload) address this.

trials.drug: Cibisatamab (CEA-TCB) + FAP-4-1BBL — bispecific T-cell engager + costimulatory

D1_biology.rationale: CEA-TCB mechanism is biologically sound — redirects T cells to CEACAM5+ tumor cells.

D2_selection.rationale: Phase I — no selection gate applied. 13.5% ORR in unselected population suggests a responder subgroup exists.

D8_systemic.rationale: CRS 57.7% — significant safety signal. Grade ≥3 CRS 3.8% is manageable but requires careful monitoring.

trials.transfer_lesson: 13.5% ORR in unselected mCRC is the highest CEACAM5 ORR in CRC to date. With Gate 2 (IO permissiveness), the responder ORR could be substantially higher. CRS management is the key safety challenge.

trials.primary_result: EU clinical hold — no efficacy data published

D8_systemic.rationale: EU clinical hold — regulatory/safety issue. No efficacy data available.

trials.transfer_lesson: Next-generation Topo1i payload (SAR445953) is the successor program. SGN-CEACAM5C EU hold does not invalidate the CEACAM5 target — it validates the need for better payload selection.

sanofi_engagement_specifics.pitch_anchor: CARMEN-LC03 D2 failure decode → two-gate solution → SAR445953 patient selection package
sanofi_engagement_specifics.key_claim: CrisPRO decoded CARMEN-LC03's failure and identified the IHC ≥80% + IO permissiveness gate. For SAR445953: CrisPRO can produce the patient selection package before the trial reads out.

what_crispro_proved: CARMEN-LC03 (Phase III, N=389): PFS HR 1.14 (P=0.82) at IHC ≥50% — D2 Selection failure decoded
CEACAM5 IHC ≥80% OS HR=0.71 (post-hoc, directional — CI not published; underpowered)
CEACAM5 50–79% PFS HR=1.38 (numerically harmful — confirms ≥50% threshold is wrong)
cCEA ≥100 ORR 41.7% vs 8.1% (P=0.003) in CEACAM5-high NSCLC — liquid biopsy proxy validated
IHC ↔ cCEA Spearman ρ=0.43 (n=92) — moderate correlation
PROCEADE-CRC-01 (N=40): ORR 7.5%; IHC Spearman rho=−0.14 (P=0.4) — IHC not predictive in CRC
CEACAM5 any-positivity in CRC: 98.7% (Jansen et al. Cancers 2024)
MSS CRC has HIGHER CEACAM5 expression than MSI-H CRC (MMR deficiency reduces CEA, P<0.0001)
what_crispro_can_offer: For any CEACAM5-targeted program: CrisPRO decoded CARMEN-LC03's D2 Selection failure and identified the two-gate framework (IHC ≥80% + IO permissiveness). For CRC programs: IHC alone is insufficient — Gate 2 is mandatory. CrisPRO can produce an ENRICH/CONSIDER/EXCLUDE stratification table, Vector-Scalpel biomarker ranking, and protocol enrollment language for any CEACAM5 program.
caveats: CEACAM5 ≥80% OS HR=0.71 is post-hoc, underpowered, CI not published — label as directional trend
cCEA ≥100 data is from NSCLC only — CRC application is by inference
CEACAM5 ≥80% prevalence in CRC (~28%) is OPEN_ASSUMPTION OA-08 — CARMEN-LC03 NSCLC proxy
CEACAM5 Target-Lock run has not been executed — EXPECTED, not COMPUTED
